name: THALASSEMIA SYNDROMES
definition: inherited disorders of hemoglobin synthesis typically seen in individuals of Mediterranean COOLEY ANEMIA = MEDITERRANEAN ANEMIA HEREDITARY LEPTOCYTOSIS = β-THALASSEMIA most severe form with trait inherited from both parents (= homozygous form) subgroup of homozygous form beta-thalassemia trait inherited from one parent (heterozygous)

demographics.Prevalence: 1% for American Blacks; 7.4% for Greek population; 10% for certain Italian
demographics.Age: develops after newborn period within first 2 years of life

dominant_finding: widening of diploic space with coarsened trabeculations and displacement ←
distribution: severe hair-on-end appearance (frontal bone, NOT inferior to internal occipital
discriminating_features: widening of diploic space with coarsened trabeculations and displacement ←
severe hair-on-end appearance (frontal bone, NOT inferior to internal occipital

general: widening of diploic space with coarsened trabeculations and displacement ←
severe hair-on-end appearance (frontal bone, NOT inferior to internal occipital
impaired pneumatization of maxillary antra + mastoid sinuses
rodent facies = ventral displacement of incisors ← marrow overgrowth in maxillary
Peripheral skeleton:: atrophy + coarsening of trabeculae ← marrow hyperplasia
widened medullary spaces with thinning of cortices
Erlenmeyer flask deformity = bulging of normally concave outline of metaphyses
premature fusion of epiphyses (10%), usually at proximal humerus + distal femur
arthropathy ← hemochromatosis + CPPD + acute gouty arthritis
regression of peripheral skeletal changes (as red marrow becomes yellow)
Chest:: cardiac enlargement + congestive heart failure ← anemia
Ribs: costal osteomas = bulbous widening of posterior aspect of ribs with thinned cortices

clinical.Rx: systematic transfusion has lessened the severity of skeletal abnormalities
